headline: In Ukraine, those who leave and those who stay: Life on hold in Sloviansk and Kramatorsk
description: 'Donbas: War of Attrition' (2/2). In this industrial region, 80% of which is occupied by Russian troops, the cities of Sloviansk and Kramatorsk have become the heart of the Ukrainian defensive system. Some of their residents choose to flee to safer areas. Others try to survive as best they can.

headline: Iran says US violated peace deal as both sides attack
Venezuela quakes kill almost 1,500, with millions more in need
'I just want to know if he's still alive': The long ordeal of families searching for loved ones lost at sea
description: Since the beginning of the year, shipwrecks in the Mediterranean and Atlantic have multiplied, leaving friends and relatives without news of those who attempt the crossing.

headline: France counts 74 drowning deaths since June 18 amid record-breaking heatwave
description: Much of western Europe has been baking under a 'heat dome' this week, and the extreme conditions have come than is usual this summer.

headline: Cultural leaders rally to save French heritage under threat
description: The launch of a heritage pass, the search for sponsors and the commitment of volunteers all point to the same effort: those working to preserve France's heritage sites are looking for new ways to avoid a looming funding gap.

headline: 250 years of American independence: Frederick Douglass, the former slave turned celebrity who skewered the Republic's hypocrisy
description: 'America 250' (6/13). In April 1876, all of Washington's elite gathered to witness the unveiling of a statue of Abraham Lincoln emancipating enslaved people. The main speaker, Frederick Douglass, himself formerly enslaved and one of the most famous faces in the country, was known for directly challenging white Americans. A legendary speech he gave in 1852 proved just that.

description: Reflective paints, green roofs, work breaks: Many African countries are seeking innovative ways to cope with increasingly extreme temperatures.

headline: Heatwave fuels frustration in Paris's northern suburbs: 'If they'd just given us shutters, we wouldn't be suffering like this'
description: Over the past week, daily life in two large concrete housing complexes in Seine-Saint-Denis has changed dramatically due to extreme heat. Residents share their struggles and anger as they try to help each other cope.

headline: French tolerance remains strong, but racism and antisemitism still run deep, report finds
description: In its annual report released on Thursday, the National Consultative Commission on Human Rights expresses concern about the persistence of xenophobic prejudices in French society, particularly among the younger generation.
